Fantasy.cozyAdaptation: Localize stakes; add magical community/rituals; make magic restorative.
Fantasy.romanceAdaptation: Use magic as obstacle/cost/metaphor for intimacy.
Fantasy.generatorInstruction: Pick magic rule + cost before selecting romance/arc.

Science Fiction.cozyAdaptation: Use repair, ecology, small crew, benevolent AI, or colony life.
Science Fiction.romanceAdaptation: Make speculative premise create romantic obstacle.
Science Fiction.breakRisks: Sci-fi as wallpaper; tech solves emotions.

Horror.cozyAdaptation: Use dread and atmosphere, keep harm restrained, end with safety restored.
Horror.romanceAdaptation: Make fear/taboo force vulnerability; protect agency and consent.
Horror.breakRisks: No dread; excessive gore in cozy; nihilism in romance.
Horror.generatorInstruction: Choose fear source + safety boundary + restoration path.

Romance.cozyAdaptation: Cozy romance emphasizes safety, community, rituals, tenderness.
Romance.romanceAdaptation: Romance primary requires central love story and optimistic ending.
Romance.breakRisks: No HEA/HFN; relationship passive or unhealthy unexamined.
Romance.generatorInstruction: Select relationship arc + emotional wound + external pressure.

Mystery.cozyAdaptation: Emphasize fair clues, village/community, off-page violence.
Mystery.breakRisks: Unfair clue; magic/tech reveal not planted.
Mystery.generatorInstruction: Choose secret + clue system + suspect web.

Thriller.cozyAdaptation: Difficult; make cozy a breather not dominant unless low-stakes thriller.

Gothic.cozyAdaptation: Dark cozy: haunted but ultimately restorative.
Gothic.romanceAdaptation: Romance entwined with threat, secrecy, and vulnerability.

Historical.cozyAdaptation: Cozy historical through village, shop, letters, domestic rituals.
Historical.breakRisks: Modern psychology with no period pressure.
Historical.generatorInstruction: Choose era pressure + social boundary + personal cost.

Crime.cozyAdaptation: Cozy crime through clever heist or low-violence caper.

Cozy.cozyAdaptation: Already core; can modify any genre via safety/repair/hope.
Cozy.romanceAdaptation: Pair with tenderness, consent, HEA/HFN if romance.
Cozy.breakRisks: No conflict; saccharine without stakes; hidden brutality.
Cozy.generatorInstruction: Choose comfort ritual + gentle pressure + restorative ending.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: The sci-fi premise creates ethical/technological pressure; romance turns it emotional; cozy restores safety.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Speculative element must cause the central problem; romance must end HEA/HFN.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Do not make the AI/tech only decoration; avoid nihilistic ending.
hybrids.queryTemplate: PrimaryGenre=Science Fiction AND CozyCompatible=Y AND RomanceCompatible=Y
hybrids.examplePremise: A guarded xenobotanist and a cheerful station mechanic must revive a failing moon garden while an anxious maintenance AI tries to keep them apart for safety reasons.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Fantasy gives wonder and rules; cozy gives community; romance gives vulnerability.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Magic must matter and relationship must be central if romance-led.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid world-ending stakes that overwhelm cozy texture.
hybrids.examplePremise: A failed spellwright inherits a sentient bakery and must partner with her childhood friend to break a town-wide luck curse before the midsummer fair.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Horror supplies dread and taboo; cozy creates safety; romance humanizes the monster/ghost.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Keep eerie atmosphere and a real boundary/taboo.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid graphic cruelty, nihilism, or romance without consent/agency.
hybrids.queryTemplate: PrimaryGenre=Horror AND CozyCompatible in (Y,Maybe) AND RomanceCompatible=Y
hybrids.examplePremise: A widowed restorer moves into a haunted lighthouse and discovers the ghost is protecting the village from a grief-fed sea curse.

hybrids.themeCore: The past can be mourned and repaired
hybrids.whyItEnhances: Gothic atmosphere deepens mystery; cozy prevents despair through repair and community.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Keep haunted past, secrecy, and architectural mood.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid making the ghost/random house irrelevant.
hybrids.examplePremise: A bookbinder cataloguing a decaying manor library finds letters from three generations of women and a ghost who keeps rearranging the shelves.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Mystery drives plot; sci-fi makes truth unstable; romance personalizes trust.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Fair clues and speculative rules must both work.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid using tech as a magic fix for clue logic.
hybrids.queryTemplate: PrimaryGenre=Science Fiction AND MysteryCompatible=Y AND RomanceCompatible=Y
hybrids.examplePremise: A memory auditor investigates a missing terraforming crew and falls for the prime suspect whose memories may have been rewritten.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Heist gives teamwork; fantasy gives rules; romance gives personal risk.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Magic must have limits; heist plan must be clever; romance must not erase agency.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid omnipotent magic solving the heist.
hybrids.examplePremise: Two rival charm-thieves join a crew to steal a saint’s bell that can expose every lie told in the capital.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Space opera gives scale; cozy focuses on crew care and local restorative missions.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Keep interstellar setting and political/cultural differences.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid making scope so large cozy rituals vanish.
hybrids.queryTemplate: PrimaryGenre=Science Fiction AND SubGenre=Space Opera AND CozyCompatible=Y
hybrids.examplePremise: A tea-ship crew resolves diplomatic disputes by matching alien ambassadors with the exact beverage their culture forgot.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Portal engine gives discovery; cozy creates refuge; character arc gives wonder meaning.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Portal world must have rules and change protagonist.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid tourism-only scenes with no transformation.
hybrids.queryTemplate: PrimaryGenre=Fantasy AND StoryArc=Voyage and Return AND CozyCompatible=Y
hybrids.examplePremise: A burned-out archivist opens a door in a laundrette and finds a village where lost socks become tiny weather spirits.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Paranormal adds hidden rules; mystery supplies puzzle; cozy supplies community and warmth.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Keep supernatural rules and clue fairness.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid random magic confession as solution.
hybrids.examplePremise: A vampire librarian and a medium baker solve the murder of a ghost who refuses to reveal how they died because it would embarrass the town choir.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Fantasy gives magical rules; gothic gives atmosphere; romance personalizes risk.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Keep academic setting, forbidden knowledge, and emotional payoff.
hybrids.queryTemplate: PrimaryGenre=Fantasy AND ToneProfile contains dark AND RomanceCompatible=Y
hybrids.examplePremise: Two rival magical linguists decode a living manuscript that edits their memories every time they lie to each other.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Sci-fi grounds ecological speculation; cozy makes restoration intimate and hopeful.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Speculative ecology must shape plot choices.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid handwaving scientific premise or removing stakes.
hybrids.examplePremise: A retired terraformer opens a moss nursery on a failing colony and discovers the planet has been communicating through scent all along.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Post-apocalypse supplies loss; cozy supplies rebuilding and mutual aid.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Keep scarcity or aftermath visible enough to justify the genre.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid relentless violence or total despair.
hybrids.examplePremise: A traveling repair cart visits settlements after a solar storm, fixing heirloom machines while stitching together a new map of safe roads.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Mystery structures plot; witchcraft adds wonder; romance deepens vulnerability.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Fair clues, meaningful magic rules, HEA/HFN if romance is primary.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid magic solving mystery without clue work.
hybrids.queryTemplate: PrimaryGenre=Fantasy AND CozyCompatible=Y AND MysteryCompatible=Y AND RomanceCompatible=Y
hybrids.examplePremise: A charm-maker accused of cursing the mayor must team with the ex who once left town to prove the curse is actually protecting someone.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Sci-fi makes the haunting technological; gothic makes tech emotionally ancestral.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Speculative mechanism and atmosphere both need causal weight.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid pure haunted-house transplant with no sci-fi logic.
hybrids.queryTemplate: PrimaryGenre=Science Fiction AND HorrorCompatible=Y AND ToneProfile contains gothic
hybrids.examplePremise: An heir to a vanished space dynasty boards the family ark and finds the ship’s AI speaking in the voices of every ancestor it failed to save.

hybrids.whyItEnhances: Fantasy/horror gives otherness; cozy gives acceptance and repair.
hybrids.mustKeepGenrePromise: Keep the monster’s difference and fear meaningful.
hybrids.avoidBecauseItBreaks: Avoid making the monster merely cute with no conflict.
hybrids.examplePremise: A village apothecary adopts a cursed bog creature whose nightmares bloom into poisonous flowers unless someone tells it bedtime stories.

rules.programmaticRule: Select exactly one PrimaryGenrePromise before layering modifiers.
rules.reason: Readers need to know which emotional/plot contract is dominant.

rules.ruleName: Romance Requires Central Love + Optimistic Ending
rules.programmaticRule: If RomanceCompatible=Y and Romance is primary, EndingFit must include HEA or HFN.
rules.reason: Romance genre convention requires central love story and optimistic resolution.
rules.breaksIf: Love story is subplot only or ends cynically.

rules.ruleName: Sci-Fi Element Must Cause Plot Pressure
rules.programmaticRule: Speculative technology, science, alien ecology, AI, time, or space condition must change choices and consequences.
rules.reason: Prevents sci-fi from becoming cosmetic set dressing.
rules.breaksIf: The same plot works unchanged in a non-sci-fi setting.

rules.ruleName: Fantasy Magic Needs Rules and Cost
rules.programmaticRule: Magic should have constraints, consequences, culture, or moral cost.
rules.reason: Rules make wonder dramatic instead of arbitrary.
rules.breaksIf: Magic solves climax without sacrifice, skill, or clue logic.

rules.ruleName: Horror Needs Dread, Boundary, or Taboo
rules.programmaticRule: Keep atmosphere, danger, uncanny boundary, or taboo even when cozy-softened.
rules.reason: Without dread, horror becomes paranormal décor.
rules.breaksIf: Threat is too cute or harmless to generate fear.

rules.ruleName: Cozy Needs Safety, Repair, and Hope
rules.programmaticRule: Limit cruelty/gore/despair and include rituals, community, emotional safety, and restoration.
rules.reason: Cozy is a reader comfort contract, not merely low stakes.
rules.breaksIf: Tone becomes brutal, nihilistic, or humiliating.

rules.programmaticRule: Solution must be inferable through planted evidence, even with magic or technology.
rules.breaksIf: Reveal depends on unrevealed magic/tech or coincidence.

rules.programmaticRule: Maintain pressure, risk, reversals, and narrowing options.
rules.reason: Thriller promise is momentum and danger.
rules.breaksIf: Cozy scenes stall urgency without changing stakes.

rules.ruleName: Character Arc Must Mirror External Engine
rules.programmaticRule: Pair plot pressure with an internal false belief or wound.

rules.ruleName: Relationship Arc Requires Agency on Both Sides
rules.programmaticRule: Both characters must make choices, have stakes, and affect the outcome.
rules.reason: Prevents objectification and passive love interests.
rules.breaksIf: One character exists only to heal or reward the other.

rules.ruleName: Cozy Horror: Soften Harm, Not Mystery
rules.programmaticRule: Use dread, atmosphere, grief, curse, or haunting; keep harm off-page or reversible/restorative where possible.
rules.reason: Maintains horror flavor and cozy comfort.
rules.breaksIf: Turns into either gore horror or toothless paranormal comedy.

rules.programmaticRule: Combine no more than one primary genre + two strong secondary engines + one tone modifier in a single concept pass.

queryGuide.filterLogic: PrimaryGenre=Science Fiction OR SciFiCompatible=Y; CozyCompatible=Y; RomanceCompatible=Y
queryGuide.recommendedStack: Story Arc + Character Arc + Relationship Arc + 2 Trope rows + Tone Modifier=Cozy + Ending=HEA/HFN
queryGuide.rejectIf: Sci-fi premise is cosmetic; romance lacks optimistic ending; cozy safety absent.

queryGuide.filterLogic: PrimaryGenre=Horror OR HorrorCompatible=Y; CozyCompatible in Y/Maybe
queryGuide.recommendedStack: Horror engine + safety rule + restoration arc + off-page harm + emotional wound
queryGuide.rejectIf: No dread; too graphic; no restored safety.

queryGuide.recommendedStack: Magic rule/cost + relationship arc + character wound + external quest/court/curse
queryGuide.rejectIf: Magic solves relationship; romance not central if sold as romance.

queryGuide.recommendedStack: Speculative rule + fair clue system + investigation arc + truth character arc
queryGuide.rejectIf: AI/tech reveals solution without planted evidence.

queryGuide.recommendedStack: Small magical community + fair clues + restorative ending + magic limits

queryGuide.filterLogic: PrimaryGenre=Gothic OR ToneProfile contains Gothic; CozyCompatible=Y
queryGuide.recommendedStack: Haunted place + family secret + grief/healing arc + restored safety

storyArcs.definition: A protagonist confronts and defeats an external threat that endangers self, home, community, or world.
storyArcs.bestgenrefits: Fantasy; Horror; Thriller; Sci-Fi; Adventure; Western
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Monster; Dark Lord; Alien Invasion; Serial Killer
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Works cozy if the monster is softened, misunderstood, contained, or defeated through community.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Avoid graphic nihilism if cozy or romance promise is primary.

storyArcs.definition: An undervalued protagonist gains status, power, love, skill, or belonging, usually after a reversal.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Secret Heir; Cinderella; Skill Awakening
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit through shop, craft, inn, garden, library, or community success.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if success is hollow and no emotional gain.

storyArcs.definition: A journey toward a concrete goal, object, cure, place, rescue, truth, or restoration.
storyArcs.bestgenrefits: Fantasy; Sci-Fi; Adventure; Cozy Fantasy; YA
storyArcs.compatibletropes: MacGuffin; Map; Artifact; Road Trip
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit when the path emphasizes wonder, hospitality, puzzles, and low-lethality danger.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks cozy when every stop escalates brutality.

storyArcs.definition: The protagonist enters a strange world, learns its rules, then returns changed or chooses a new home.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Portal World; Hidden City; Alien Planet
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Excellent cozy fit when the strange world is wondrous but emotionally safe.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if the world is only set dressing and does not change the character.

storyArcs.definition: Confusion, mismatched desires, disguises, or social pressure build until truth restores or improves community order.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Mistaken Identity; Dinner Party Disaster; Odd Couple
storyArcs.compatiblerelationshiparcs: Enemies to lovers; Friends to lovers
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Core cozy engine when conflict remains humane and resolution restores belonging.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if humiliation becomes cruelty.

storyArcs.definition: A character flaw, obsession, lie, or corrupting desire leads to ruin.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Deal with Devil; Corrupt Power; Fatal Secret
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Low cozy fit; can be backstory or antagonist arc in cozy.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks romance if main couple gets nihilistic ending.

storyArcs.definition: A frozen, wounded, cursed, or morally lost character is restored through trial, love, truth, or sacrifice.
storyArcs.bestgenrefits: Romance; Fantasy; Cozy; Historical; Literary; Horror
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Curse Breaking; Second Chance; Haunted Past
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Excellent cozy fit: restoration is a core cozy promise.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if transformation is unearned or purely cosmetic.

storyArcs.definition: A hidden truth is pursued through clues, suspects, interviews, memory, forensics, or pattern recognition.
storyArcs.bestgenrefits: Mystery; Thriller; Sci-Fi; Cozy Mystery; Gothic
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Red Herring; Cold Case; Unreliable Witness
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit when investigation emphasizes puzzle, community, and fair clues over gore.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks mystery if clues are unfair or reveal depends on coincidence.

storyArcs.definition: The protagonist must endure, flee, outwit, or survive a hostile environment, captor, disaster, or system.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Locked In; Hostile Planet; Siege
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Can be cozy-adjacent only if danger is softened and support systems matter.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks cozy with prolonged cruelty.

storyArcs.definition: A character or community must be saved from captivity, danger, curse, illness, or isolation.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Kidnapping; Cure Quest; Missing Person
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit when rescue is restorative rather than militaristic.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if rescued character has no agency.

storyArcs.definition: A wronged protagonist seeks revenge, then either transforms it into justice or is consumed by it.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Vengeance Quest; Betrayal; Murdered Loved One
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy use only as softened backstory or justice-with-mercy arc.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks cozy/romance if vengeance is glorified cruelty.

storyArcs.definition: A team assembles to steal, retrieve, sabotage, or expose something under constraints.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: One Last Job; Specialist Team; Double Cross
storyArcs.compatiblerelationshiparcs: Crew found family; Rivals to allies
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit when cleverness and community replace violence.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if puzzle logic is ignored.

storyArcs.definition: The protagonist resists, exposes, or overturns an oppressive institution.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Resistance Cell; Propaganda; Chosen Symbol
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit as local, humane reform rather than mass war.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if the system is vague and stakes are generic.

storyArcs.definition: A wounded person, place, family, town, ecosystem, or relationship is repaired.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Inherited Shop; Magical Garden; Community Project
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Core cozy engine.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if no meaningful wound or repair exists.

storyArcs.definition: A character seeks or discovers knowledge that creates moral, cosmic, political, or intimate danger.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Forbidden Book; AI Secret; Ancient Lab
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit if danger is eerie and manageable rather than nihilistic.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if information solves everything without cost.

storyArcs.definition: The protagonist changes form, role, identity, power, class, species, or worldview.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: New Powers; Shapeshift; Cyborg Upgrade
storyArcs.compatiblecharacterarcs: Identity Arc; Positive Change; Monster Within
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit when acceptance and integration are central.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if transformation has no identity consequences.

storyArcs.definition: A character joins, resists, reforms, or escapes a tribe, school, guild, crew, court, company, or cult.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Magic School; Space Crew; Secret Society
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit through guild, academy, cooperative, library, inn, or village.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if institution has no rules or social cost.

storyArcs.definition: Two contrasting characters must cooperate and change each other.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Odd Couple; Forced Proximity; Partners
storyArcs.compatiblerelationshiparcs: Friends to lovers; Rivals to allies
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Excellent cozy and hybrid engine.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if one partner is passive or disposable.

storyArcs.definition: A character crosses a life threshold: adolescence, grief, parenthood, aging, vocation, exile, or loss of innocence.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: First Love; Leaving Home; Trial
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit if growth leads to belonging and agency.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if lesson is preachy rather than dramatized.

storyArcs.arcname: Out of the Bottle / Wish Lesson
storyArcs.definition: A granted wish, magic, technology, or shortcut gives power with rules and unintended consequences.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Wish Magic; Time Loop; Device Gone Wrong
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit through whimsical consequences and moral lesson.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if rules are arbitrary.

storyArcs.definition: An ordinary person is thrust into extraordinary danger and must improvise.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Wrong Place Wrong Time; Ordinary Hero
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit only if danger is low/mid and support exists.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if protagonist never makes choices.

storyArcs.definition: A person with exceptional power must decide how to use it and what responsibility costs.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Secret Identity; Training; Public Symbol
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit with low-scale, local heroics.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if power has no limits or cost.

storyArcs.definition: A confined location, monster/threat, and sin/violation create escalating peril.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Haunted House; Creature Feature; Lab Accident
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy horror variant: house is eerie, threat is containable, resolution restores safety.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks horror if threat lacks dread; breaks cozy if cruelty dominates.

storyArcs.definition: The central engine is not just who did it, but why a morally shocking act happened.
storyArcs.compatibletropes: Hidden Motive; Psychological Reveal
storyArcs.cozyadaptation: Cozy fit if motive is emotionally legible and violence is off-page.
storyArcs.breakrisk: Breaks if motive is random.

characterArcs.definition: Starts with a limiting lie, faces pressure, accepts a healthier truth, and acts differently at the climax.
The protagonist already knows the truth and changes the world or other characters by holding firm.
The protagonist rejects truth, embraces a destructive lie, and loses self, love, power, or life.
An initially decent character slowly accepts unethical compromises until becoming antagonist or antihero.
A guilty, complicit, or fallen character takes responsibility and makes reparative choices.
The character loses a false ideal and gains a harder, truer understanding of the world.
A wounded character learns to live, love, trust, or belong again.
characterArcs.bestgenrefits: Romance; Cozy; Contemporary; Fantasy; Horror Romance
characterArcs.compatibletropes: Found Family; Protective Haunting; Second Chance

characterArcs.definition: A young or inexperienced character gains agency, maturity, identity, and moral awareness.
The character stops seeking worth through approval, status, usefulness, or sacrifice.
A guarded character learns discernment and vulnerability without surrendering agency.
A powerful or privileged character accepts duty without martyrdom or control.
A controlled character claims voice, boundaries, vocation, and choice.
An outsider discovers or builds a home without erasing their difference.
characterArcs.compatibletropes: Found Family; Hidden World; New Town

characterArcs.definition: A character with frightening power, appetite, curse, or violence integrates restraint and identity.
A bereaved character moves from stuck grief into meaningful action, connection, or remembrance.
A skeptical character learns to accept magic, love, community, faith, or mystery.
A sheltered character gains worldly wisdom without losing compassion.
A hyper-controlled character learns collaboration, uncertainty, and emotional openness.
characterArcs.compatibletropes: AI Malfunction; Road Trip; Forced Proximity

characterArcs.definition: A fearful character chooses meaningful risk despite fear.
A character who resists authority learns to build, protect, and guide others responsibly.

relationshipArcs.definition: Opponents move from antagonism to respect, vulnerability, and committed love.
relationshipArcs.genresafetyrule: Must end emotionally satisfying if romance is primary.

relationshipArcs.definition: Existing affection becomes romantic risk, desire, and chosen partnership.
relationshipArcs.genresafetyrule: Works well in cozy due to safety and warmth.

relationshipArcs.definition: Former lovers or estranged partners confront old wounds and choose mature reconnection.
Characters who would avoid each other must share space, mission, ship, inn, lab, or crisis.
relationshipArcs.compatibletropes: Only One Bed; Trapped on Spaceship
relationshipArcs.genresafetyrule: Enhances hybrids by turning plot pressure into emotional pressure.

relationshipArcs.definition: A performative alliance becomes emotionally truthful.
relationshipArcs.genresafetyrule: Works when deception has consequences and consent stays clear.

relationshipArcs.definition: Competing characters learn complementary strengths and shared purpose.
A group of outsiders becomes chosen kin through care, loyalty, and shared trials.
Wisdom, skill, ethics, or power passes between generations or roles, often with conflict.
relationshipArcs.genresafetyrule: Breaks if mentor solves all problems.

relationshipArcs.definition: One character protects another, then must learn agency, equality, and mutual care.
Relatives confront history, secrets, guilt, or inheritance to repair or redefine bonds.
relationshipArcs.genresafetyrule: Cozy if repair is possible; gothic if past is oppressive.

relationshipArcs.definition: Investigator and suspect orbit truth, mistrust, attraction, and power imbalance.
relationshipArcs.genresafetyrule: Romance requires ethical handling and fair agency.

relationshipArcs.definition: A human and AI, alien, monster, ghost, fae, or android form a bond that tests identity.
relationshipArcs.bestgenrefits: Sci-Fi; Fantasy; Paranormal Romance; Horror Romance
relationshipArcs.genresafetyrule: Enhances genre if otherness is thematic, not cosmetic.

relationshipArcs.definition: A protagonist repairs not just one bond but a village, crew, shop, colony, or household network.
A damaged bond undergoes truth, accountability, restitution, and a possible renewed future.
Contrasting temperaments create conflict then complementarity.
